focus: Q1: What is the exact mechanism by which pending embeddings become failed? Trace enforceRetryRetentionLimits() in retry-manager.js: which rows are parked by pending-cap vs max-age, in what order, and against which embedding_status set?

graphEvents.label: dist retry-manager.js matches TypeScript retention behavior
Max-age retention parks old pending/retry rows first
Pending-cap retention parks remaining queue overflow after max-age pruning
Current backlog would overflow the default cap by 9239 pending/retry rows
runtime JS mirrors source retention SQL
max-age update precedes cap overflow selection
both branches filter embedding_status IN pending/retry
default cap would park remaining rows after queue rank 1000

label: Retention parking is a pre-drain failure writer: max-age first, pending-cap second, status set pending/retry only
Runtime dist retry-manager.js mirrors the source retention SQL and exported function
Live failed rows all carry max-age retention reason; default cap would currently overflow 9239 pending/retry rows
enforceRetryRetentionLimits only parks current pending/retry rows; success, failed, and partial rows are outside both retention filters
Current DB counts: failed=7105, pending=10179, retry=60, success=9623; failed reason distribution is 7105 max-age and zero pending-cap

direction: Pending-cap caused the observed failed set
reason: Live failed rows all have failure_reason='Retry retention max age exceeded'; pending-cap remains a future risk under default cap but did not create the current failed rows.
